rulings.Case Title: CAROL ANN LEVINE VS. JEFFREY BRANDSTETTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Dismissal [Ccp 473(B)]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 22, 2011, Line 2, PLAINTIFF CAROL LEVINE's Motion For Relief From Dismissal [CCP 473(B)]. The motion is transferred to Department 610 to be heard on January 12, 2012 at 2:00 p.m. Department 610 issued the dismissal and therefore, that is the department that will hear this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CHICAGO TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. BURL STEVEN WALKER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 22, 2011, Line 3, DEFENDANTs WALKER, BURL STEVEN, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE TO BEATRICE C WALKER UNDER THAT DECLARATION OF TRUST DATED 2/5/2004'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained as to first and second causes of action, with 15 days leave to amend. Overruled as to third cause of action. As to first cause of action, sustained with leave to amend to allege that funds were received by a defendant for use and benefit of plaintiff. As to second cause of action, sustained with leave to amend to allege that defendant's representation was not true, that he had no reasonable grounds for believing the representation wasn't true when he made it, and that plaintiff relied on the representation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: WESLEY STERMAN VS. NICHOLAS COLYVAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Leave To File Amended Answer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 22, 2011, Line 6, DEFENDANT NICHOLAS COLYVAS' Motion For Leave To File Amended Answer To Complaint. The motion is granted on the following conditions, which are imposed in light of the current trial date and discovery cut-off. Plaintiff shall have up to and including January 3, 2012 to propound written discovery on the newly alleged defense of statute of frauds. Defendant must agree to accept electronic and/or fax service of such discovery. Service of this discovery shall be considered complete upon emailing or faxing, with no additional days added. Defendant must respond to such discovery within 15 days after service, as set forth in this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: STACEY ZIELENSKI VS. PARC 55 HOTEL MANAGEMENT, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories And Pltfs'S Second Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 22, 2011, Line 3, PLAINTIFF STACEY ZIELENSKI Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories And Pltfs'S Second Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents - The Court grants this motion. Defendant shall provide complete objection-free responses and $2,000 in sanctions by January 23, 2012. Even though Plaintiff has yet to amend the complaint, the propounded discovery meets the "relevant to the subject matter" standard of CCP 2017.010. See Stewart v. Colonial Western Agency, Inc., (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 1006, 1013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MM
